---
title: ""
title-block-banner: false
page-layout: full
toc: false
format:
  html:
    title-block-style: none
---

::: {.eg-page}

::: {.eg-hero}
::: {.eg-wrap}

## Evidence, not estimates, for how landscapes are changing.

::: {.eg-lede}
We combine remote sensing, ecological modeling, and social-ecological systems research to answer one question for donors, conservancies, and governments across the region: is this land-use or climate claim actually true — and can we prove it over time?
:::

::: {.eg-hero-ctas}
[View services](#services){.eg-btn .eg-btn-solid} [Start a project](#contact){.eg-btn .eg-btn-outline}
:::

:::
:::

::: {#services .eg-section .eg-services}
::: {.eg-wrap}
::: {.eg-sec-head}
[Services]{.eg-eyebrow}

## Five layers of analysis, stacked around one landscape.

Each service reads like a layer in a GIS project — independently useful, and stronger together. Pick one, or let us build the full stack for a project.
:::

::: {.eg-layer-stack}

::: {.eg-layer}
::: {.eg-layer-marker}
[ ]{.eg-layer-toggle .eg-l1}

[LAYER 01]{.eg-layer-index}
:::

::: {.eg-layer-body}
::: {.eg-layer-main}
### Digital MRV & Carbon Verification

[Independent, satellite-grounded MRV for carbon and biodiversity credit projects]{.eg-layer-one-liner .eg-l1}

::: {.eg-layer-desc}
We build and audit measurement-reporting-verification pipelines — biomass estimation, land-cover change detection, time-series monitoring — to registry standard, so credits hold up to scrutiny and due diligence holds up to buyers.
:::

::: {.eg-layer-meta}
[Who it's for]{.eg-tag-label}

[Project developers]{.eg-chip} [Conservancies]{.eg-chip} [Impact investors]{.eg-chip}

::: {.eg-layer-coord}
02°10'S 38°04'E\
Illustrative anchor: Tsavo–Kasigau region
:::
:::
:::

::: {.eg-layer-image}
![Satellite view of forest canopy near the Tsavo–Kasigau corridor](images/DataCleaning.png)
:::
:::
:::

::: {.eg-layer}
::: {.eg-layer-marker}
[ ]{.eg-layer-toggle .eg-l2}

[LAYER 02]{.eg-layer-index}
:::

::: {.eg-layer-body}
::: {.eg-layer-main}
### Land-Use & Land-Cover Change Modeling

[Mapping and forecasting how agriculture, settlement, and forest cover are moving]{.eg-layer-one-liner .eg-l2}

::: {.eg-layer-desc}
Historical change detection and scenario modeling for deforestation, agricultural expansion, and urban growth — built for spatial planning, safeguard compliance, and donor reporting.
:::

::: {.eg-layer-meta}
[Who it's for]{.eg-tag-label}

[County governments]{.eg-chip} [Land-use planners]{.eg-chip} [Conservation NGOs]{.eg-chip}

::: {.eg-layer-coord}
00°49'S 35°42'E\
Illustrative anchor: Mau Forest Complex
:::
:::
:::

::: {.eg-layer-image}
![Aerial view of forest edge and farmland near the Mau Forest Complex](images/DataCleaning.png)
:::
:::
:::

::: {.eg-layer}
::: {.eg-layer-marker}
[ ]{.eg-layer-toggle .eg-l3}

[LAYER 03]{.eg-layer-index}
:::

::: {.eg-layer-body}
::: {.eg-layer-main}
### Landscape Connectivity & Conservation Planning

[Habitat fragmentation, wildlife corridors, and human-wildlife conflict — mapped and modeled]{.eg-layer-one-liner .eg-l3}

::: {.eg-layer-desc}
Connectivity analysis and protected-area effectiveness assessments that show where conservation investment and community agreements will actually hold, not just where a map says they should.
:::

::: {.eg-layer-meta}
[Who it's for]{.eg-tag-label}

[Wildlife conservancies]{.eg-chip} [Park authorities]{.eg-chip} [Conservation NGOs]{.eg-chip}

::: {.eg-layer-coord}
00°02'N 37°33'E\
Illustrative anchor: Northern Rangelands
:::
:::
:::

::: {.eg-layer-image}
![Wildlife corridor landscape in Kenya's Northern Rangelands](images/DataCleaning.png)
:::
:::
:::

::: {.eg-layer}
::: {.eg-layer-marker}
[ ]{.eg-layer-toggle .eg-l4}

[LAYER 04]{.eg-layer-index}
:::

::: {.eg-layer-body}
::: {.eg-layer-main}
### Social-Ecological Systems & Resilience Assessment

[Where land, climate, and community governance meet — analyzed together, not separately]{.eg-layer-one-liner .eg-l4}

::: {.eg-layer-desc}
We pair spatial data with social science to assess resilience, land governance, and community-based natural resource management — the layer most geospatial firms skip, and most funders now expect.
:::

::: {.eg-layer-meta}
[Who it's for]{.eg-tag-label}

[Research consortia]{.eg-chip} [Bilateral donors]{.eg-chip} [Conservancy associations]{.eg-chip}

::: {.eg-layer-coord}
03°04'S 37°21'E\
Illustrative anchor: Kilimanjaro socio-ecological zone
:::
:::
:::

::: {.eg-layer-image}
![Community grazing land near Kilimanjaro's socio-ecological zone](images/DataCleaning.png)
:::
:::
:::

::: {.eg-layer}
::: {.eg-layer-marker}
[ ]{.eg-layer-toggle .eg-l5}

[LAYER 05]{.eg-layer-index}
:::

::: {.eg-layer-body}
::: {.eg-layer-main}
### Climate Adaptation & Food-Security Modeling

[Drought, rangeland, and yield forecasting for the people who plan around them]{.eg-layer-one-liner .eg-l5}

::: {.eg-layer-desc}
Spatiotemporal modeling of drought risk, pastoral resilience, and agricultural yield, built to support early-warning systems and index-based insurance design.
:::

::: {.eg-layer-meta}
[Who it's for]{.eg-tag-label}

[Insurers & reinsurers]{.eg-chip} [Early-warning programs]{.eg-chip} [Agtech]{.eg-chip}

::: {.eg-layer-coord}
02°17'N 37°59'E\
Illustrative anchor: Marsabit, Northern Kenya
:::
:::
:::

::: {.eg-layer-image}
![Dry rangeland landscape near Marsabit, Northern Kenya](images/DataCleaning.png)
:::
:::
:::

:::
:::
:::

::: {#approach .eg-section}
::: {.eg-wrap}
::: {.eg-sec-head}
[Approach]{.eg-eyebrow}

## Three things every layer is built on.
:::

::: {.eg-approach-grid}
::: {.eg-approach-item}
[A]{.eg-num}

### Field-grounded

Every model is checked against ground truth — plots, transects, or partner verification — not just pixels.
:::

::: {.eg-approach-item}
[B]{.eg-num}

### Community-informed

Social-ecological methods mean local governance and knowledge shape the analysis, not just the output.
:::

::: {.eg-approach-item}
[C]{.eg-num}

### Registry-ready

Work is documented to the standard carbon registries, donors, and auditors actually require — no rework at review time.
:::
:::
:::
:::

::: {#sectors .eg-section .eg-sectors}
::: {.eg-wrap}
::: {.eg-sec-head}
[Sectors]{.eg-eyebrow}

## Who we build these layers for
:::

[Carbon & Conservation Finance]{.eg-sector-chip} [Government & County Planning]{.eg-sector-chip} [Multilaterals & Bilateral Donors]{.eg-sector-chip} [Research Institutions]{.eg-sector-chip} [Agriculture & Insurance]{.eg-sector-chip}
:::
:::

::: {#contact .eg-section .eg-contact}
::: {.eg-contact-wrap}
::: {.eg-contact-left}
## Have a landscape that needs a closer look?

Tell us what you're trying to prove, protect, or plan for. We'll tell you which layers apply.
:::

::: {.eg-contact-box}
[EMAIL]{.eg-mono-label}

[hello@equatorgeospatial.com](mailto:hello@equatorgeospatial.com){.eg-email}

[BASED IN]{.eg-mono-label}

[Nairobi, Kenya — serving East Africa]{.eg-email-sub}

[Send us a brief](mailto:hello@equatorgeospatial.com){.eg-btn .eg-btn-solid}
:::
:::
:::


:::
